reasoning: The Yahoo Finance fetch tool failed, so I am relying on the web search and base rate principles. HPQ had a 14% drop in late May 2025 following a cut in annual profit forecasts due to tariffs. However, stock prices generally follow a random walk over short horizons, with past predictable news largely priced in. Without reliable recent trend data to show a very strong signal, we must stick closely to the base rate of ~0.5. I am setting the probabilities at 0.51 for short-term and slightly scaling to 0.52 for the longest term (to account for a tiny expected positive drift of equities), while remaining exceptionally close to 50/50 as weekly/monthly moves are unpredictable.
